column1: Open Schools - Independent School Roles and Responsibilities - 4/17/25

column2: Purpose
column4: Accountabilities

column2: Ensures the school is fully enrolled by the first day and that the enrollment process is smooth, accessible, and family-friendly
column4: Oversees student recruitment efforts.
Plans and leads school tours and open houses.
Coordinates and finalizes enrollment decisions.
Oversees collection and processing of registration paperwork.
Oversees financial aid decisions and tiered tuition processes. Manages related vendors (e.g., FAST, TADS)
Manages partnerships with local early childhood education placement agencies.
Maintains and updates the school’s enrollment strategy documentation and reviews with the board, annually.
Maintains documentation for approach to tuition and tiered tuition/financial aid and reviews with the board, annually.
Tracks long-term enrollment trends and advises school in adjusting recruitment accordingly
Manages summer school enrollment

column2: Complies with applicable assessment requirements and uses data to inform instruction
column4: Maintains assessment calendar.
Oversees student assessment processes and trains staff in implementation
Coordinates the review and interpretation of student assessment data

column2: Supports development and sustainability of an effective independent school board
column4: Supports board meeting prep: agendas, packets, minutes
Provides regular updates to the board on the school’s mission, vision, goals, and metrics
Drafts board resolutions for review and approval
Maintains board archives
(See Board accountabilities for roles that sit with a board and may be filled by a TL)

column2: Ensures that prospective families, staff, and the broader community are informed about the school. Maintains the school’s brand identity
column4: Manages the school’s social media accounts.
Manages and updates the school website.
Updates and monitors external school review sites (e.g., Google Reviews, GreatSchools).
Promotes the school through enrollment, summer programs, and fundraising campaigns.
Prepares and distributes the school newsletter.
Oversees branding consistency in public-facing materials

column2: Ensures the school remains compliant with all legal and regulatory requirements
column4: Maintains business licenses, charitable registrations, and official organizational documents

column2: Ensures the school implements an authentic, high-quality Montessori program
column4: Oversees staff professional development planning and tracking.
Plans and facilitates regular staff meetings.
Orders replacement materials, furniture, and classroom supplies.
Designs and coordinates summer school programming.
Ensures compliance with state education standards (if applicable)

column2: Ensures student records and information systems are properly maintained and up to date
column4: Manages student records such as enrollment and academic data using SIS (e.g. Transparent Classroom, Power School, Infinite Campus, etc.).
Maintains student forms required for licensure (e.g., emergency contacts, immunization records).
Collects and updates family contact information.
Develops and maintains data privacy policies.

column2: Ensures school practices are aligned with the Wildflower equity principle and promotes inclusion and fairness throughout the community
column4: Stewards equity policies and practices for the school

column2: Ensures the school environment is safe, well-maintained, and secure
column4: Maintains regular communication with the landlord
Oversees lease renewal process
Manages facility maintenance and repairs
Oversees cleaning
Ensures all required permits are current and inspections are completed on time
Oversees facility-related vendors (e.g., utilities, waste management, landscaping)
Orders and restocks office supplies and paper goods

column2: Ensures that families feel welcomed, informed, and engaged in the school community
column4: Maintains and publishes the school calendar
Welcomes new families to the school
Plans and hosts family events (educational and celebratory)
Schedules and coordinates regular family conferences
Coordinates family volunteer activities
Coordinates family surveys and feedback loops
Engages with alumni and the broader community

column2: Ensures the school is financially sustainable and in compliance with fiscal regulations
column4: Oversees the bookkeeper, accountant, and any related systems (e.g., QuickBooks, Bill.com)
Creates and monitors the budget
Manages the cash flow forecast
Prepares financial reports for the board
Oversees banking operations, including bank accounts and credit cards (as applicable)
Oversees the bill payment process
Oversees revenue collection, including tuition and state subsidies
Processes payroll and manages the relationship with the payroll vendor. Ensures 1099s and W-2s are issued annually
Oversees loan payments and reporting
Files required nonprofit financial reports (e.g., annual 990, quarterly 941)
Issues 1099s to contractors
Manages petty cash, if applicable
Maintains and updates fiscal policies as needed

column2: Ensures that snacks and meals are provided to students when offered and meet program standards
column4: Manages relationships with meal and snack vendors
Purchases food and kitchen supplies
Submits documentation for food reimbursement programs

column2: Ensures the school secures the philanthropic and donor support necessary for long-term financial sustainability
column4: Leads fundraising efforts and manages donor relationships
Issues donor acknowledgments and tax-compliant gift receipts
Monitors and tracks the use of restricted funds, when applicable

column2: Ensures the school is appropriately staffed, complies with HR regulations, and provides fair and equitable compensation and benefits
column4: Recruits staff members and oversees hiring process
Ensures staff files are up to date (background checks, admin certifications, contracts)
Maintains employee handbook and ensures policy compliance
Selects and manages staff benefits (e.g., health insurance, retirement plans)
Maintains the compensation policy and reviews it with the board periodically
Manages employee relations and ensures compliance with labor laws
Manages relationships with extracurricular and afterschool program vendors

column2: Ensures the school is protected by appropriate legal, insurance, and risk management practices
column4: Secures and maintains school insurance policies
Consults legal counsel when necessary
Creates and manages release of liability forms (e.g., field trip forms)

column2: Ensures the school meets and maintains all licensing requirements, if applicable
column4: Maintains ongoing communication with the licensing office
Ensures compliance with annual licensing requirements (e.g., training hours, student-to-staff ratios)

column2: Ensures a safe, healthy environment that supports student wellness and well-being
column4: Maintains student and family handbook.
Maintains safety plans emergency operating procedures.
Ensures first aid kits are stocked and accessible
Schedules fire drills and oversees safety inspections
Oversees student behavior and discipline processes

column2: Ensures high-quality, data-informed student services aligned with student needs and diagnoses to support an inclusive learning environment
column4: Develops and oversees relationships with any student support services (OT, PT, SSC, Special Educator, nurse, etc.).

column2: Ensures the school has the necessary technology to support instructional and operational needs
column4: Procures and maintains school technology, including hardware and software

column2: Ensure adult community is healthy and operating in a teal organizational structure.
column4: Stewards self-management policies and practices for the school

column2: Serve as liaison with Wildflower network
column4: Ensures compliance with the Wildflower membership agreement
Participates in pod.
Completes The Wildflower Foundation's school survey
